subject: Human Resources
title: The Rise of HR Technology
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on The Rise of HR Technology.

hook.text: In the 1990s, HR still ran on paper files and spreadsheets. One payroll error could take days to fix.

buildup.text: Enterprise software like PeopleSoft and SAP HR began digitizing employee records, payroll, and benefits.

discovery.text: HRIS platforms centralized data that was previously scattered across filing cabinets and managers' notebooks.

twist.text: Early HR software was expensive and clunky. Many HR professionals resisted it, preferring their paper systems.

climax.text: By the 2000s, digital HR was standard. Cloud platforms made it accessible even to small businesses.

punchline.text: HR went from filing cabinets to cloud in two decades.

quiz.question: What was the first major HR technology shift?
options: Enterprise software digitizing employee records and payroll
